title: Full release: New Export Gantt to PDF feature!
excerpt:
New feature update…:sparkles: Export Gantt to PDF  :sparkles: 
  
This month we are bringing to you a new and improved Gantt widget export experience! The exported PDF will display the entire Gantt chart without certain …

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
New feature update… Export Gantt to PDF

Screen Shot 2023-03-21 at 5.32.17 pm980×566 38.6 KB

This month we are bringing to you a new and improved Gantt widget export experience! The exported PDF will display the entire Gantt chart without certain parts being cut off - even allowing you to scroll through the finished export for the complete experience!

https://global.discourse-cdn.com/monday/original/3X/4/b/4be96d18c2fe1696cdcd6998bb702e8ecc14a132.mov

To note:

  • Currently, there is a limit of 300 items in one export.

  • Depending on the width of the screen, there is a limit in the timeframe that is supported in the Gantt, this will be between 2-6 years.

We will be gradually releasing this feature so keep an eye out

How do you feel about this feature update? We want to hear your feedback!

posts.author: palford (Paul Alford)
posts.cookedHtml: How soon will this be available please, will it be in month March 2023?

posts.author: hrix (Heather Rix)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an ETA on this feature?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @hrix and @palford,

I’ve just reached out to our Product Team for a release update! Stay tuned

posts.author: palford (Paul Alford)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @BiancaT

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for your patience, I have just received an update from our product team - this feature will be fully released to all accounts before the end of the month so do keep an eye out in the next few weeks! CC @hrix

posts.author: biograf (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT Dear Bianca, this is so perfect for us and I think for many other people
Please let me know, as we are on an EU server, if the ETA will be the same as for other users?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are so glad to hear this (and are also very excited for the release)!

That said, I believe the ETA is the same across all accounts in all regions however I will double check for you and circle back here if the ETA is different

posts.author: YERIO (Ioannis Yerolatsitis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dear Bianca and team, what do you mean by a limit of 300 items? What do you count as items?
We are currently getting only error prompts that “Gantt exceeds supported width for PDF export”.
When are you planning to increase this limit?
PDF does not need to be limited to A4 page…Hope that this update can be released fast to relief our team from the numerous complaints.
PS: Test with 2-3 entries looks great though! Much more professional than the previous spreadsheet view.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @YERIO!

Thanks for your feedback I have shared your questions up with our product team and will circle back here when I have some answers for you. Thanks for your patience

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @YERIO, I have recently heard back from our product team who have responded with the following details

We have recently updated the item limit to 640 items which includes subitems and any other item included in the export (not items filtered out).

As for the width, this isn’t something we are planning on increasing this at this time, however there are a few ways you can workaround this:

  • Collapsing the workspace left-hand pane will also increase the width
  • Collapsing the gantt left hand pane (where names/items are listed will increase the width)
  • The size of the widget will affect the width- widget should be docked for full size
  • Users can zoom out on the window to increase the width

Finally, regarding limiting PDF to A4 page, are you referring to the document exporting on multiple pages? If so, I am afraid this this supported at this time, however something they might consider developing later down the track.

Hope this information helps!!

posts.author: YERIO (Ioannis Yerolatsitis)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for the workarounds @BiancaT
Hopefully this will be solved in the near future, as the subitems and tasks are not shown when the left pane is hidden, and we can only see the information within the Gantt.
Looking forward to the future evolutions and making this cleaner.

Side note, the dependencies between tasks are omitted when exported. Is this planned to be supported in the future?

Thank you once again

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for your understanding @YERIO! Regarding dependencies, I have followed up with our developer team and transparently this isn’t on our immediate roadmap, however we do encourage you to submit this as a feature request so that we can grow visibility and votes to get this on their radar. Every quarter we take the features with the top votes to our development team for a response

posts.author: mellyandthejets (Melissa Ward)
posts.cookedHtml: has this feature been released yet?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! This feature has been fully released

posts.author: msaunders (Mark Saunders)
posts.cookedHtml: It seems to currently cut the page at the fold - is this just a screenshot exported to PDF? Is there a way to get a full project if it happens to be multiple screens in length?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @msaunders, there are a few limitations regarding the export.

Currently,

  • There is a limit on the width of the gantt that is supported in an export- the limit depends on screen resolution, and is between 3-6 years. If the gantt widget width is narrower, this will affect the width- to achieve max width, we encourage you to have the gantt docked.

  • The Gantt will be auto-fitted as part of the export to be able to capture the most info in the gantt, meaning that the zoom scale may change during the export- e.g. if users are using ‘month’ scale, it may change to ‘quarter’.

Additionally at this time, the export doesn’t include multiple pages. That said, the team will consider developing later down the track and based on user feedback Thanks for your understanding in advance!

posts.author: AndreaNZSIR (Andrea)
posts.cookedHtml: This a great update, any chance we can export a PDF without the groups expanded?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @AndreaNZSIR,

If I am understanding correctly, you should be able to close the groups using the arrow outlined in the screenshot below prior to exporting:

Screen Shot 2023-05-09 at 12.20.17 pm748×918 44.8 KB

Is this what you’re referring to?

posts.author: AndreaNZSIR (Andrea)
posts.cookedHtml:
Screenshot 2023-05-09 143906754×816 55.6 KB

No, I mean this one to the left. If you have the groups in collapsed view, when you click ‘export to PDF’ it will automatically expand them all and print the expanded view.

posts.author: biograf (Chris)
posts.cookedHtml: dear AndreaNZSIR, you can use a filter for the groups which you want to see. The Export will only show the groups you see with active filter.

posts.author: PM-aholic (PM-aholic)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Bianca… thanks for sharing this! I am a power user of project management/scheduling software tools over the years but this is my first time using Monday.com at a new organization. I really like the collaboration features but there are some gaps in gantt chart capabilities, so I am happy to see recent enhancements in this area.

A couple questions:
1 - you mention ability to export up to 300 items, but my PDF export only ever shows what I can fit onto the screen to display. Are you able to clarify how to do this?
2 - is there any workaround, or fix in work to resolve the item names being truncated? Ex: in your video, the item name “Video recording & editing softw…” gets cut off. It would be nice to expand that pane to show longer task names.

Thanks for all your help!

PM-aholic

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @PM-aholic,

Happy to clarify a few things for you!

To start the limit has actually increased to 640 items - this only counts items shown in the gantt and not those that have been filtered out. There is a limit on the width of the gantt and this limit does depend on screen resolution which might be impacting the export… We encourage users to dock the widget to achieve max width.

In regard to the item name being cut off, I am afraid this is expected if it’s a lengthy name. We will certainly share your feedback with our team internally, however we encourage you to share your feedback via this form link → gantt pdf export feedback form

posts.author: ongjin (jin)
posts.cookedHtml: Im having the same issue where the text in the left hand side menu getting truncated. would be great if that could be expanded. The other issue is really poor resolution when i try to export the gantt chart.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for sharing with this with - I will add this to the comments I’ve already shared

It would be great if you can also fill out the feedback form above as this feedback goes directly to our product team - the more feedback, the better!

posts.author: Lucas1 (Lucas)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely an improvement, but hoped this would also finally show the dependencies…

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
We hear you and are aware of this limitation

Please share this via the form!

posts.author: Cotters (Martin Cottingham)
posts.cookedHtml:
I was struggling with this because there is more than one way to export, and I was using the wrong one. Having located the new Export button, I can confirm that this is exactly what I need and is 100% better than before.
Thanks Monday and @BiancaT for bringing this out.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: We really appreciate your feedback Martin - thank you

posts.author: ECO_Blair (Blair Cole)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a timeline as to when the Gantt export will include Dependencies? The new dependency update is great, but now being able to show these on an export would give Monday.com a great improvement.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Blair,

I am afraid we do not have a confirmed timeline regarding dependencies in the Gantt export, this is currently a known limitation. I apologise for the setback and will certainly circle back to this post if we are informed of any changes

posts.author: KevinHolmes (KevinH)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking forward to dependencies being visible, but I also have another request that would improve the export function.

I have renamed a number of my Gantt charts, however, when I export i get a simple ‘Gantt’ as the title rather than the title that appears on my board. Could this be fed back?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Kevin!

Can you confirm where you’re exporting from? Are you exporting an entire Dashboard or a specific Gantt within the Dashboard? That said, in all scenarios I’ve tested, the title name (that I’ve renamed) exports correctly… This sounds like it could potentially be a bug.

Does this same behaviour persist in a private window?

posts.author: Ghazal (Ghazaleh Hafizi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

How can I customize my Gantt’s export to PDF? I would like to adjust the time I like to show in my presentation! I have tried zooming, changing it to Month but it pops back to quarterly every time and exports it to pdf! It also cuts off the last row everytime!

posts.author: KevinHolmes (KevinH)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca

I think there is a bug in here somewhere!

If I export from 1 I get an unnamed gantt and if I export from 2 I get the title of the board rather than the name of the Gantt. Examples below.

I would have expected both methods to be titles ‘Pay Gantt’

image001.png1903×501 104 KB

From method 1

image002.png965×282 52.9 KB

From method 2

image003.png966×323 66.8 KB

I hope this helps.

Kevin

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Ghazal,

At this time it isn’t possible to customise the gantt for exporting purposes. I understand the value in a feature like this, so encourage you to submit this as feature request so our community can vote and help bring this feature to life

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for this information Kevin!!

I have actually just tested this via the methods you have shared, and believe that this is expected…

If you export via method 2, this is exporting from the board level, so will include the board title. If you export via method 1, you’re exporting on the widget level, and as such your customised gantt title should correctly export.

Can you please undock your gantt widget and rename the gantt as you please. Can you then try exporting via method 1, docked or not? Does the name appear correctly?

Screen Shot 2023-10-05 at 11.59.24 am1624×334 42.9 KB

Screen Shot 2023-10-05 at 11.59.37 am966×430 17.8 KB

I look forward to hearing from you!

posts.author: KevinHolmes (KevinH)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Bianca

Undocking, that’s the solution!

When I created the Gantt views they were docked and renamed at the top line where all the views are listed. When I undock and rename, the exported PDF now adopts the name and shows on the PDF itself.

I believe that this should work using both methods, so it probably is a small bug. As I now have a work around, I’m happy!

Thanks for looking into this,

Kevin
